surface_class_identifiers.description: An authored configure preset that defines or inherits invocation configuration for a build lane.
An authored build preset that realizes a configure preset through target selection and config-aware invocation.
An authored test preset that realizes a configure preset through governed test invocation.
An authored toolchain file under cmake/toolchains that constrains architecture or system assumptions for a lane.
An authored helper surface under cmake/ide that governs projection generation or toolchain-family mapping.
An authored CI or automation consumer that invokes named preset lanes without creating new lane meaning.
An authored developer-facing task or editor entrypoint that consumes visible preset lanes.
A generated IDE output, manifest, descriptor, or other downstream projection surface.

status_categories.description: An authored surface that is authoritative within the preset/toolchain layer while remaining downstream of build graph lock.
A generated or downstream surface that may mirror the matrix but may not author it.
A retained compatibility or bridge surface whose relationship to a more stable lane must stay explicit.
A retained but frozen surface that is not the default lane and must not silently expand in authority.
A surface whose use or interpretation carries support-policy, release-policy, or externally consequential meaning that later prompts must govern explicitly.

platform_arch_toolchain_linkage_categori.description: Lane meaning is expressed directly through authored cache-variable bindings in configure presets.
Lane meaning is anchored to an explicit CMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE reference.
Lane meaning is tied to DOM_PROJECTION_ID and authored projection helper rules rather than to product runtime shipping status.
Lane meaning aligns directly with a canonical target-matrix row or release-support row.
Lane meaning aligns only as a family projection or provisional environment lane and must not be overread as a shipped release commitment.
A CI, editor, or script surface consumes preset meaning through explicit preset selection rather than creating new platform or product taxonomy.

observed_surface_families.notes: Visible operator-facing lanes used by humans, CI, and editor tasks.
Authored scaffold presets that support inheritance and de-duplication without becoming the full operator contract.
Authored toolchain modules constrain architecture-family lanes without defining target taxonomy.
Authored projection helpers govern how projection presets and manifests are generated.
Projection-oriented configure presets are authored and valid, but they remain projection lanes rather than universal operator or shipping canon.
Compatibility and nostalgia lanes are retained with explicit legacy posture and must not silently become default support commitments.
Alternate toolchain or backend lanes are authored and real, but later support-policy and release-policy prompts must decide how far they count as official release realizations.
These surfaces consume the consolidated matrix and verify alignment, but they do not author preset canon.
Derived projection outputs may mirror preset-toolchain state but may not replace authored law.
These surfaces are real and authored, but their full release and publication meaning belongs to later Υ prompts.

derived_only: generated IDE project trees under ide/
canonical_vs_derived_status_rules.rule_note: Canonical within this layer does not mean upstream of build graph lock. Generated or projected surfaces may mirror preset law but may not author it.

runtime_vocabulary_alignment_notes: Kernel meaning is not defined by any preset family or compiler lane.
Component meaning is not defined by any single toolchain or IDE projection lane.
Service meaning is not defined by any build invocation path.
Product identity is not identical to a preset, platform lane, or toolchain file.
consolidation_rules: Visible operator lanes remain the default human-facing contract and must stay aligned with CI and editor consumers.
Hidden scaffold presets may remain canonical support surfaces without becoming the full operator contract.
Projection presets remain projection-oriented even when authored and validated.
Legacy or best-effort lanes must carry explicit transitional or deprecated status rather than silent parity with primary lanes.
Release-labeled lanes may exist before full release doctrine is frozen, but they remain review-gated for later prompts.
Preset or toolchain names may not redefine product identity, target class, or semantic ontology.
Generated descriptors and generated projection outputs may not stand in for authored preset/toolchain law.

validation_audit_categories.description: Verify that configure, build, and test preset surfaces remain aligned where contract lanes require symmetry.
Verify that CI, editor tasks, and release dry-run entrypoints consume the same visible preset meanings.
Verify that projection regeneration remains confined to projection outputs and does not mutate authoritative surfaces silently.
Verify that release-labeled lanes carry explicit release markers such as DOM_BUILD_KIND where later prompts require them.
Verify that preset families and toolchain families map explicitly to target-matrix and toolchain-matrix expectations.
Verify that generated descriptors, build trees, and IDE outputs do not replace authored law.

preset_toolchain_specific: Consolidation must be extracted from repo reality and Υ-0 build graph lock rather than invented as greenfield cleanup.
Visible invocation convenience does not create authority.
Projection outputs and configured descriptors remain derived even when they are easier for tools to parse.
CI and editor consumers may consume preset meaning but may not redefine release or support policy.

downstream_refinement_prompts.reason: Versioning constitution needs stable lane and environment meaning.
Release-index and resolution work needs one interpretation of release-facing preset families.
Release contract profile needs stable product-platform-toolchain realization semantics.
Build reproducibility work consumes the consolidated matrix and its deterministic linkage surfaces.
Archive and distribution work must consume rather than reinvent lane meaning.
Artifact and target naming work must share the same consolidated lane model.
Later operator-control-plane prompts must rely on stable preset-toolchain interpretation during checkpoint reassessment.
